# vary

[![NPM Version][npm-image]][npm-url]
[![NPM Downloads][downloads-image]][downloads-url]
[![Node.js Version][node-version-image]][node-version-url]
[![Build Status][travis-image]][travis-url]
[![Test Coverage][coveralls-image]][coveralls-url]

Manipulate the HTTP Vary header

## Installation

```sh
$ npm install vary
```

## API

```js
var vary = require('vary')
```

### vary(res, field)

Adds the given header `field` to the `Vary` response header of `res`.
This can be a string field name or an array of string field names.

```js
// Adds the field "Origin" to the Vary header of the response
vary(res, 'Origin')
```

### vary.append(header, field)

Applies the given header `field` to the `Vary` header string `header`.
This can be a string field name or an array of string field names.

```js
// Get header string appending "Origin" to "Accept, User-Agent"
vary.append('Accept, User-Agent', 'Origin')
```

## Examples

### Using with Express.js

When using this module with Express.js, you can use it as a middleware to
automatically add fields to the Vary header based on the request headers.

```js
var express = require('express')
var vary = require('vary')

var app = express()

// Add the Origin request header to the Vary response header
app.use(function (req, res, next) {
  // Add Origin to Vary header so proper responses are cached
  vary(res, 'Origin')
  next()
})

app.get('/', function (req, res) {
  // Handle request
  res.send('Hello, World!')
})

app.listen(3000)
```

### Using with Content Negotiation

When performing content negotiation, the Vary header is essential for proper
caching. This module can be used to easily add the necessary headers to the
Vary header.

```js
var express = require('express')
var vary = require('vary')

var app = express()

// Add the Accept request header to the Vary response header
app.use(function (req, res, next) {
  // Add Accept to Vary header so proper responses are cached
  vary(res, 'Accept')
  next()
})

app.get('/', function (req, res) {
  // Negotiate the response based on the accept header
  res.format({
    'text/plain': function () {
      res.send('Hello, World!')
    },
    'text/html': function () {
      res.send('<p>Hello, World!</p>')
    },
    'application/json': function () {
      res.json({ message: 'Hello, World!' })
    },
    default: function () {
      res.status(406).send('Not Acceptable')
    }
  })
})

app.listen(3000)
```

## Description

The Vary HTTP response header determines how to match future request headers
to decide whether a cached response can be used rather than requesting a
fresh one from the origin server. It is used by the server to indicate which
headers it used when selecting a representation of a resource in a content
negotiation algorithm.

The vary module provides a function to manipulate the Vary header of a response:

- If the header is not set, it will set it with the given field name
- If the header is already set, it will append the given field name to the
  existing header

This module will automatically ensure that the Vary header is properly
formatted and does not contain duplicates.
